_comment: Tools and tests available to a community health nurse (MLHP/MPW) at a Sub Health Centre upgraded to Health and Wellness Centre (SHC-HWC) in rural West Bengal. Source: Ayushman Bharat Operational Guidelines for Health and Wellness Centers (MoHFW, GOI) — Annexures 3 and 4. The Diagnosis Agent is constrained to suggest only these when generating bedside observations and discriminating tests. See docs/bedside_tools_crosscheck.md for the full guideline citation table.

basic_instruments.measures: height (cm) — for BMI and paediatric growth assessment
MUAC (mid-upper arm circumference), head circumference, abdominal girth
throat inspection, pupil reactivity, skin and wound examination
breath sounds, heart sounds, bowel sounds, fetal heart sounds
peak expiratory flow rate (L/min) — for asthma and COPD assessment
visual acuity — distance and near vision
bronchodilator response — relevant for wheeze and respiratory distress assessment

rapid_diagnostic_tests.detects: dengue NS1 antigen (early) and dengue IgM/IgG (later phase)

rapid_diagnostic_tests.tool: HIV rapid test (whole blood finger prick)
rapid_diagnostic_tests.detects: HIV antibodies — two-test algorithm as per NACO protocol
sample collected by nurse and sent to PHC designated microscopy centre for AFB smear; nurse cannot perform microscopy at SHC level

not_available: AFB sputum microscopy (SHC can collect sample only; microscopy done at PHC)
RPR / rapid syphilis serology (PHC level only)
